title: Friday Sermon 21 June 2002
description:
Today’s sermon is on the Divine attribute of al-Noor (The Light):
 
Noor is that light which helps to see.  

Noor connotes the worldly light that assists sight and also discernment or insight. 

Allah has called Himself the Noor of the heavens and the Earth in 'The Holy Quran' for He is the One who bestows light profusely.

(Abul Qasim al-Hussayn ibn Mufaddal ibn Muhammad al-Raghib. 'Al Mufradat fi Gharib al-Quran').
 
The identification of true believers is that they come from darkness towards light and that they discover a positive change in their condition.

(Hazrat al-Hajj Hakeem Maulana Nooruddin – Khalifatul Masih I.  'Haqaiq al-Furqan'.  407, 408).

It is not possible to have fear of God and to shun this world and to come into light from darkness without belief and that the means to achieve this belief is the Word of God:
 
… it is not possible to have perfect fear of God; without certainty, it is not possible to tread  along the delicate paths of righteousness and to purify one’s conduct of all traces of  ostentations.  Similarly, it is not possible without this certainty to reject worldly riches and  honour, and be heedless of the favour of kings and to believe in God alone as one’s treasure.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Nuzool al-Masih'.  Ziya al-Islam, 1902.  'Ruhani Khazain: xviii'.  470).
 
The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) said that 'The Holy Quran' is a Book with  guidance and light …

(Ahmad.  'Karamatus Sadiqin'.  Punjab Press, 1893.  'R. K: vii'.  56).

Allah the Exalted bestowed two exclusive lights to the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) that had not been granted to anyone before him and they are: Surah al-Fatiha [The Opening] and the last verses of Surah al-Baqarah [The Heifer]:*
 
… there can be no station higher than attaining the love of God.  Who is more worthy of proclaiming himself the light than he who leads to God’s love?  It is for this reason that the Lord of Honour and Glory has named the Holy Prophet 'the light'.  He says: 
  
O People of the Book!  There has come to you Our Messenger who unfolds to you much of what you had kept hidden of the Book and overlooks much.  There has come to you now from Allah a Light and a clarifying Scripture.**

(Ahmad.  'Sirajuddin Isai kei Char Sawalon kei Jawab' ['Four Questions by Mr. Sirajuddin, a Christian, Answered'].  Ziyaul Islam, 1897.  'R. K: xii'.  372. English: 1968, 2009.  55).

Narrated Abdullah ibn Amr: The Messenger of Allah said ‘Indeed Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, created His creation in darkness, then He cast His Light upon them, so whoever is touched by that light he is guided, and whoever is not, he goes astray.  It is for this reason that I say that the pens have dried with Allah's knowledge.

(Abu Isa Muhammad ibn Isa al-Tirmidhi.  'Jami al-Tirmidhi').
 
The difference between light and darkness is that there is discernment in light whereas darkness is devoid of it.

(Nooruddin.  'Haqaiq: ii'.  139).
 
When Allah grants spiritual light to a person, they are as if bestowed a sort of purity and this  distinguishes them from those who wander in the darkness of ignorance.

(Ahmad.  'Aina e kamalat e Islam'.  Riyaz e Hind, 1893.  'R. K: v'. 99).

It is narrated on the authority of Abu Huraira that a dark-complexioned lady (or youth) used to  sweep the mosque.  The Messenger of Allah ﷺ missed her (or him) and inquired about her (or him).  The people told him that she (or he) had died.  He asked why they did not inform him, and it appears as if they had treated her (or him) or her (or his) affairs as of little account.  He (the Holy Prophet) said: Lead me to her (or his) grave.  They led him to that place and he said prayer over her (or him) and then remarked ‘Verily, these graves are full of darkness for their dwellers.  Verily, the Mighty and Glorious Allah illuminates them for their occupants by reason of my prayer over them.

(Muslim).

* Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj.  'Sahih Muslim'.  
** 'The Holy Quran'.  Al-Maidah [The Banquet]: 16.

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20020621-UR.pdf
